body.title: CLOSED: Epic: capability extraction — move everything we can out of Igor into the datacenter
body.result: All children closed. SensorDevice (last open child) shipped in e9bfc53. pe_chain stays in Igor by design. SMART disk deferred to follow-on ticket.
body.description:
**Epic / tracking ticket. Children file as opportunities surface.**

Per Akien architectural North Star (2026-05-01): the datacenter is the agent-as-pieces (capability appliances), Igor is the master reasoning model. The substrate is reusable; cognition is Igor's. Any agent can be built on top of the datacenter; Igor is one tenant.

## The split

- **Datacenter** (`/home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/`): capability appliances — inference, web_server, postgres, browser_use, swadl, discord_bot, the rack itself. Akien's cross-project lab. Reusable substrate for any agent.
- **Igor** (`/home/akien/TheIgors/wild_igor/igor/`): master reasoning model. Cognition only. Runs ON the datacenter; doesn't CONTAIN the datacenter.
- **TheIgors/lab/**: Igor-specific tooling and design docs only. Cross-project tooling moves out (to `/home/akien/dev/src/ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop/` per T-claudeandakien-workshop-evolution).

## Why

- **Easier to debug Igor.** Cognition isolated from substrate concerns.
- **Capabilities debuggable in isolation.** Each appliance is its own scope, smaller surface.
- **Reusability.** Future agents (Igor variants, agents in other projects) inherit the substrate for free.
- **Reduces complexity.** The currently-tangled monorepo splits into clean tiers.

## Children (file as opportunities arise — partial list)

- [ ] T-inference-migrate-igor-to-datacenter-device (M, filed 2026-05-01) — Igor inference via comms://inference bus envelope.
- [x] Web server already in datacenter (Phase 4 shipped commit 3850610).
- [x] Browser-use already in datacenter (commit f3c48ec).
- [x] SWADL already in datacenter (commit 3850610).
- [x] Discord bot already in datacenter (commit 3850610).
- [x] Postgres device already in datacenter (Phase 1 shipped).
- [ ] Reading pipeline → datacenter device? (TBD — design call)
- [ ] Cortex / memory engine → datacenter device? (TBD — high-inertia, design call needed)
- [ ] Pe_chain (autonomous editor) → datacenter device? (TBD — entangled with Igor's reasoning loop)
- [ ] Sensor tree → datacenter device? (TBD)
- [ ] Forensic logger → datacenter device? (TBD)

## Children NOT to move (likely)

- Cognition primitives (NE, TWM, milieu, BG, basal-ganglia rules, attractors) — these ARE Igor.
- Engram graph / habit graph — these ARE Igor.
- Reasoning workflow + voice_ab — these ARE Igor.

## Sprint approach

This ticket stays open as a tracker. Each child gets its own ticket and is sprinted independently. When a child closes, mark the checkbox here. When a non-trivial number of items have moved, snapshot progress in a slate or design doc.

**Affected files:** N/A directly — children carry their own file lists. This ticket is the umbrella.

**Design rules:** N/A directly — children carry their own. Pattern across children: cleanly separable boundary, capability surface as a device interface, igor consumes via comms:// bus envelope.

**Scope boundary:** IN — extracting cleanly-extractable capabilities from Igor into datacenter devices. OUT — Igor's cognition (NE/TWM/milieu/BG/engrams stay); rebuilding Igor on the datacenter substrate (different ticket); creating new agents that use the substrate (different ticket).

**Test plan:** N/A directly — each child carries its own. Pattern: each extracted capability has both unit tests in its datacenter device + integration tests in Igor showing the bus-routed call works equivalent to the legacy direct call.

## Scope expansion (2026-05-01, per D-claudeandakien-folded-2026-05-01)
ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop is obsolete; everything that was workshop-bound is agent_datacenter material. Beyond utility_closet/ (the original scope), this ticket now also covers:
- lab/claudecode/cc_skills/ — per-skill triage (cross-machine vs Igor-internal); cross-machine ones migrate to agent_datacenter master skills home (see T-skills-master-home-in-datacenter for design)
- Any other formerly-workshop-bound modules surfaced during the migration
Coordination: T-skills-master-home-in-datacenter ships first (design + deploy mechanism); this ticket then executes the per-skill moves.
